evidence_quotes.quote: Addictive Design & Dark Patterns: Many games use manipulative interfaces to drive microtransactions or dependencies, while still maintaining full control to later pull the product.
unjust practice of removing access to purchased digital games commonly referred to as "killing games."
Unfair Commercial Practices: The removal of purchased content without adequate warning or compensation is a deceptive practice and should fall under the scope of this initiative.

paragraphs.text: I welcome this initiative and strongly support the EU's effort to enhance digital fairness and close the gaps in online consumer protection. As part of this effort, I urge the European Commission to explicitly address a growing concern in the digital entertainment sector: the unjust practice of removing access to purchased digital games commonly referred to as "killing games." Key Concerns Loss of Paid Content: Consumers increasingly face situations where games or other digital products they legally purchased are rendered inaccessible due to discontinued support, server shutdowns, or revoked licenses. This undermines the very notion of ownership and trust in digital markets. Lack of Transparency: Game publishers often do not provide clear or prominent information that games require ongoing online access to function or that their availability is time-limited. No Recourse or Refunds: Consumers typically have no way to back up or preserve these games, and receive no compensation when access is revoked violating principles of fairness and legal clarity. Alignment with Initiative Objectives Unfair Commercial Practices: The removal of purchased content without adequate warning or compensation is a deceptive practice and should fall under the scope of this initiative. Addictive Design & Dark Patterns: Many games use manipulative interfaces to drive microtransactions or dependencies, while still maintaining full control to later pull the product. This compounds the harm to consumers. Fairness and Level Playing Field: Smaller developers and ethical platforms offering DRM-free or preservation-friendly games are disadvantaged compared to major players with anti-consumer lock-ins. Enforcement should encourage sustainable digital ownership models. Proposed Additions Digital Ownership Safeguards: Introduce a right to retain access to purchased digital products, or at minimum a guaranteed preservation window and refund options upon service termination. Transparency Requirements: Mandate clear disclosure of any time-limited access, online-only dependencies, or potential delisting risks at point of sale. Preservation Rights: Support interoperability and archival efforts, allowing users or third parties to preserve and run legally acquired software post-shutdown. Conclusion Digital fairness must include the right to enjoy, preserve, and rely on purchased digital products. Without strong protection, consumers are left vulnerable to abusive practices that damage trust in the digital single market. I hope the Commission will consider these aspects when finalising this initiative.
